name: Centro Campesino, Farmworkers Center, Inc
description_short: Community development nonprofit offering free homeownership counseling, housing rehabilitation, and financial assistance to low-income families and farmworkers in Florida.
description_long:
Centro Campesino Farmworkers Center, Inc. was founded in 1972 by farmworkers and advocates responding to deplorable housing and working conditions affecting migrant and seasonal farmworkers in South Florida. The organization evolved from a farmworker advocacy group into a comprehensive Community Development Corporation serving low-income families across Central and South Florida. Over five decades, Centro Campesino has built substantial community impact through direct service delivery rather than commercial lending.

Centro Campesino offers a diverse portfolio of free and low-cost services focused on housing stability and economic advancement. Services include homebuyer and homeowner counseling for distressed homeowners, housing rehabilitation through their Weatherization Assistance and Retrofit programs, affordable rental properties, nonprofit homebuilding, job training and placement, youth education programs, and emergency assistance. They operate multiple affordable housing developments including Biscayne Senior Housing, Florida City Village, and Palm Villas, managing over 130 rental units. Their homeownership solutions specifically serve both prospective homebuyers and families facing foreclosure.

Centro Campesino's distinguishing characteristics include their deep community roots, specialized focus on farmworker populations, and track record of tangible outcomes: constructing over 500 single-family homes, rehabilitating 6,000+ homes, assisting 3,000+ families through homeownership, and helping 2,000+ distressed homeowners since 2008. They are a chartered NeighborWorks America member, United Way partner agency, and National Council of la Raza affiliate with continuous Miami-Dade County operations since 1972. Their programs address interconnected barriers to financial stability including housing access, employment, education, and disaster recovery.

Centro Campesino is genuinely suited for low-income families and farmworkers seeking non-predatory housing support and financial counseling. The primary caveat is that they operate primarily in Florida (Central and South Florida counties) and focus on housing-related services rather than comprehensive credit repair or personal lending. Their emergency assistance and job training programs suggest broader financial support capacity, though specific eligibility requirements and service availability by location are not detailed on their website.
pros: Free homeownership counseling and education for prospective and distressed homeowners
Affordable rental housing with 130+ units across multiple properties
Weatherization and home retrofit programs for low-income homeowners
Specialized services for farmworkers and migrant families
Free job training and placement for youth and farmworker adults (1,000+ served)
Direct disaster recovery and hurricane relief services
Member of NeighborWorks America and partner with United Way—indicating strong nonprofit credentials
50+ year track record with measurable impact (500+ homes built, 6,000+ rehabilitated)
cons: Services limited to Central and South Florida counties only; geographic reach is restricted
Website does not specify eligibility requirements, income limits, or application processes
No details on response times, wait lists, or availability of emergency assistance funds
Housing-focused mission means limited scope for credit repair, debt relief, or personal financial counseling outside housing context
Website lacks specific information about homeownership counseling curriculum or foreclosure prevention program details
best_for: Low-income homebuyers and first-time homeowners in Central/South Florida seeking free counseling
Farmworker and migrant families needing affordable housing and economic advancement support
Homeowners facing foreclosure or housing instability in Miami-Dade and surrounding counties
Low-income families seeking home rehabilitation, weatherization, or rental assistance
diagnosis: Centro Campesino is best for low-income families, farmworkers, and first-time homebuyers in Central and South Florida seeking free, non-predatory housing counseling, affordable rentals, or home rehabilitation. The main caveat is geographic limitation to Florida and a housing-focused mission; they are not a credit repair, debt settlement, or personal lending service.
services: Foreclosure prevention and distressed homeowner assistance
Weatherization Assistance Program for energy-efficient home upgrades
Nonprofit homebuilding and single-family home construction
Job training and employment placement services
Youth education programs (YouthPride, STEM Jobs Initiative, Project-Based Learning)
Disaster recovery and hurricane relief services
Property management for affordable housing developments
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
